Items.Title: Git 命令（以简化您的开发🤗） | Git Commands ( to ease your development 🤗 )
Items.Description:
在这个博客中，我将分享一些基本命令的技巧，以使使用 git 更容易。

要从 git 开始，我们需要了解在使用 git 时事情是如何在系统本地工作的。

该图将有助于理解在本地使用 git 时实际发生的情况。 从工作区开始，工作区是git没有处理的文件的地方，它是保存所有未跟踪文件的地方。 暂存区的文件将成为下一次提交的一部分。 存储库包含所有项目提交。 使用存储库，我们可以在某个时间点拥有文件。

您需要做的第一步是初始化存储库。

此命令可帮助您设置分别用于提交的用户名和用户电子邮件地址。

 git config -- global user . name "Shivam164" git config -- global user . email "skpoooo6@gmail.com"

该命令用于查看工作区和暂存区的状态。

此命令用于将文件添加到暂存区。

要将工作区中未跟踪的所有文件带到暂存区，我们使用：

这里 '。' 指定暂存所有未跟踪的文件。

要指定应该跟踪的文件，我们可以使用它们的文件名：

仅用于暂存 abc.txt。

通常，我们会在每次提交时保留一条短消息，以便将来了解该提交以及其他人了解。

这里的'-m'是消息。

在某些情况下，您可能无法用某些语言来解释您的工作，即使在这种情况下，我们也不应该只在一行中写一条非常大的消息，因为在 GitHub 上阅读这样的消息会很困难。 对于暂存文件后的这种情况，我们不只是像上面那样直接提交。 我们写：

当我们在没有 -m 的情况下编写 git commit 时。 您将看到一个名为 Vim 的控制台文本编辑器。

现在你可以开始在这里写你的提交信息了，你只需要按 I (INSERT)。 即使在长消息中，你也应该在开始到长消息之前保留一个短行，然后在一个行空间之后，你可以写你的长消息。 当你写完你的信息后，你可以先按 'ESC' 然后按 ':wq' 保存并退出来关闭这个 vim。

其他有用的 git commit 命令之一：

 git commit -- amend - m "commit message'

这个命令将把这个提交与最后一个提交合并，并且会有一个新的提交消息。

有很多方法可以查看提交历史：

  • 最基本的一个：显示从头到尾的所有提交，以及与提交相关的所有信息。

     git log

    它显示基于 HEAD 的提交。

  • 要查看一些最后提交的数量：

     git log - 5

    查看最后 5 次提交。

  • 查看从某个提交到某个其他提交的提交：我们使用这些提交的哈希值。

     git log af156ff . .32509 d0

  • 要查看提交的统计信息：

     git log -- stat

  • 最方便的 git log 命令之一：

     git log -- all -- oneline -- graph -- decorate

    查看哈希值为 1c16945 和 9dcff6 的提交之间的区别。

    我们也可以直接使用。

    这将显示工作区和目录的文件的差异。

    我们可以使用 --staged with git diff

    这将显示工作区和暂存区文件的差异。

    只有暂存区中的文件可以被隐藏。

    使用 git stash 后，如果我们使用命令 git status 我们将不会在暂存区看到任何文件。

    我们还可以在使用 git stash 时给出一条消息，这样如果我们以后需要它，我们就可以回忆起我们对隐藏文件所做的事情。

     git stash save "changed title"

    要查看所有隐藏的更改：

    要查看一些特定的存储，假设索引 1

     git stash show - p stash @ { 1 }

    要检索存储更改：

    • 检索所有隐藏的更改
    • 使用特定索引检索存储更改

    要删除隐藏的更改：

    • 删除一些特定的

    创建新分支：

    • 从 HEAD 当前指向的分支：

       git branch newBranch

    • 从 HEAD 当前未指向的分支：

       git branch newBranch fromBranch

    • 创建一个新分支并切换到它：

       git checkout - b newBranch fromBranch

    切换到不同的分支：

     git checkout toSwitchBranchName

    比较不同的分支：

     git diff master .. new_feature

    重命名分支：

    要更改分支的名称，HEAD 当前指向：

     git branch - m newBranchName

    要更改分支的名称而不考虑 HEAD：

     git branch - m oldName newName

    删除分支

    git branch - d branch_to_delete

    • 如果我们当前在我们试图删除的分支上，我们会得到错误。

      所以我们必须确保我们不在我们想要删除的分支上。

    • 如果我们要删除的分支未合并并且有提交，则此命令将不起作用。 所以我们需要使用'-D'而不是'-d'

    目前它只有与本地处理文件有关的命令。 我很快就会更新它。 👊 👊



原文:

Git is a prevalent version control system ( VCS ) used by most programmers. In this blog, I am going to share some tips with basic commands to make using git easier.

To start with git, we need to understand how things work locally on the system while using git.

This diagram will help to understand, what actually happens while using git locally. Starting with the working area, the working area is where the files that are not handled by git, it is the place where all the untracked files are kept. Staging area has files that are going to be part of the next commit. The repository contains all the project commits. Using the repository we can have files as it was at some point in time.

Git Commands:

1. Initializing git in your directory

The very first step that you need to do is to initialize the repository.

2. git config

This command helps you to set the username and user email address to be used with your commits respectively.

git config --global user.name "Shivam164"
git config --global user.email "skpoooo6@gmail.com"

3. git status

This command is used to see the state of the working area and staging area.

3. git add

This command is used to add files to the staging area.

To take all the files that are untracked in the working area to the staging area, we use:

Here ‘.’ specifies to stage all the untracked files.

To specify files that should be tracked, we can use their file name:

In order to stage abc.txt only.

4. git commit

The git commit command captures a snapshot of the project’s staged changes. Generally, we keep a short message with each commit in order to understand that commit in the future as well as for other people to understand.

⇒ It is good to keep commits in the present tense and short ( about 50 characters ).

git commit -m "first commit"

Here ‘-m’ is for the message.

There can be cases when you cannot explain your work in some words, even in such a scenario we should not just write a very big message in a single line, because that will be tough to read such messages on GitHub. For such a scenario after staging some file, we don't just directly commit like above. We write:

When we write git commit without -m. You will see a console text editor known as Vim.

Now you can start writing your commit message here, you just need to press I (INSERT). And even within the long message, you should keep a short line at the start to before the long message, then after a line space, you can write your long message. When you are done writing your message, you can close this vim by first pressing ‘ESC’ and then ‘:wq’ to save and quit.

One of other useful git commit command:

git commit --amend -m "commit message'

This command will merge this commit with the last commit and it will have a new commit message.

5. git log

The git log command displays all of the commits in a repository’s history.

There are many ways to see commit history :

  • Most basic one: Shows all the commits from end to start, with all the info related to commits.

    git log

    It shows commits based on the HEAD.

  • To see some number of the last commits:

    git log -5

    To see the last 5 commits.

  • To see commit from some commit to some other commit: We use the hash value of those commits.

    git log af156ff..32509d0

  • To see the stat of the commits:

    git log --stat

  • One of the handiest git log commands:

    git log --all --oneline --graph --decorate

    ⇒ all: to see all the commits

    ⇒ oneline: to keep all the data of commits within one line.

    ⇒ graph: to keep commits in graphical form, very useful when working with branches.

    ⇒ decorate: to make it visually better.

6. git show

The git show command will help to see details of a particular commit.

7. git diff

The git diff command is to see the difference between different commits.

To see the difference between commit with hash value 1c16945 and 9dcff6.

We can also use it directly.

This will display the difference in files of the working area and directory.

We can use - - staged with git diff

This will display the difference in files of the working area and staging area.

8. git stash

The git stash temporarily shelves (or stashes) changes you’ve made to your working copy so you can work on something else, and then come back and re-apply them later on.

Only the files that are in the staging area can be stashed.

After using git stash, if we use the command git status we will not see any file in the staging area.

We can also give a message while using git stash so that if we need it later we will be able to recall what we had done with the stashed files.

git stash save "changed title"

To view all the stashed changes:

To view some particular stash, let’s say with index 1

git stash show -p stash@{1}

To retrieves stash changes:

  • To retrieve all the stashed changes
  • To retrieve stash change with particular index

To delete stashed changes:

  • To deleted some specific ones

9. git branch

The git branch command is used to work with branches in git.

Creating new branch:

  • From the branch where HEAD is currently pointing to:

    git branch newBranch

  • From a branch where HEAD is not currently pointing to:

    git branch newBranch fromBranch

  • Creating a new branch and switching to it:

    git checkout -b newBranch fromBranch

Switching to different branch:

git checkout toSwitchBranchName

Comparing different branches:

git diff master..new_feature

Renaming branches:

To change the name of the branch, HEAD is currently pointing to:

git branch -m newBranchName

To change the name of the branch irrespective of HEAD:

git branch -m oldName newName

Deleting branches

git branch -d branch_to_delete

  • If we are currently on the branch that we are trying to delete, we will get error.

    So we have to make sure that we are not on branch we want to delete.

  • If the branch we want to delete is unmerged and has commits than this command won’t work. Git does this to stop accidental deletion of branches.

    So we need to use ‘-D’ instead of ‘-d’

Currently it has commands only related to handling files locally. I will be updating it soon. 👊

Items.Title: 为什么估算是浪费 | Why Estimates Are Waste
Items.Description:
软件开发是不可预测的，但人们每周都会浪费数小时来猜测任务需要多长时间。

估计可以为未来的变化提供信心，但这不适用于软件开发，因为：

  • 如果您可以预测工作，那么您应该将其自动化而不是重复它。
  • 如果您无法预测工作，那么尝试这样做是浪费时间。

自动化代替准确估计

重复增加了可预测性。 如果您从未完成过某项任务，则无法准确估计该任务需要多长时间。 换句话说，你重复的任务越多，它就越容易预测。

重复的工作应该是自动化的。 在软件开发中，重用库、框架和 API 的能力意味着可以自动化可重复的工作。 您重复的次数越多，代码重用和自动化就会节省更多的精力。

准确的估计与浪费的努力有关。 随着重复次数的增加，自动化的价值和可预测性都会上升。 所以 …

不准确的估计是有害的

如果估计的目的是围绕未来进行计划，那么不准确的估计只会导致问题。 估算工作时间和精力的成本加剧了这一点。

与其花费时间和精力来估计无法预测或应该自动化的工作，我们应该寻找替代方案。 最敏捷的方法之一是在没有估计的情况下工作。

没有估计的工作

没有估计的工作对于良好的软件开发至关重要，并且与持续交付齐头并进。 无需估计即可工作的最简单过程是：

  1. 确定最有价值的任务。
  2. 完成这项任务。
  3. 重复。

重要的是要考虑估算工作所花费的时间及其提供的价值。 这个最小的过程对你来说可能太严格了，但下次你评估你的工作时，请考虑它提供的价值。

如果您想进一步讨论这个问题，请在Twitter @BenTorvo或电子邮件ben@torvo.com.au上与我联系



原文:

Software development is unpredictable but people waste hours every week trying to guess how long tasks will take.

Estimation can provide confidence in future changes, but this doesn’t apply to software development because:

  • If you can predict work then you should automate it instead of repeating it.
  • If you can’t predict work then attempting to do so is a waste of time.

Automation Instead of Accurate Estimation

Repetition increases predictability. It isn’t possible to accurately estimate how long a task will take if you have never done it. In other words, the more you repeat a task the more predictable it becomes.

Repeated work should be automated. In software development the ability to re-use libraries, frameworks, and APIs means that repeatable work can be automated. The more you repeat it the more effort would be saved with code re-use and automation.

Accurate Estimates Correlate with Wasted Effort. As repetitions increase the value of automating and the predictability both go up. Therefore …

Inaccurate Estimation is Harmful

If the purpose of estimation is to plan around the future then inaccurate estimates can only lead to problems. This is compounded by the cost of estimating work in time and effort.

Instead of spending time and effort on estimating work that can’t be predicted or should be automated we should find alternatives. One of the most agile approaches is to work without estimates.

Working Without Estimates

Working without estimates is critical to good software development and goes hand in hand with continuous delivery. The simplest process to work without estimates is:

  1. Identify the most valuable task.
  2. Work on that task.
  3. Repeat.

It is important to consider the time spent on estimating work and the value it provides. This minimal process may be too strict for you but next time you are estimating your work consider the value it provides.

Items.Title: 如何提高工作效率并处理时间管理 | How to be productive and deal with time management
Items.Description:
嗨，我叫 Julia，我是一名 30 多岁的自学成才的辅助功能和前端开发人员，现在也在帮助其他人将其融入科技领域。

我的追随者经常问我如何安排我的一天并确保我保持高效。 此外，最近在几个 Twitter 空间中讨论了这个话题。 似乎很多开发人员都在为这些事情苦苦挣扎。

那么让我告诉你我的故事。

目录

但首先，让我告诉你一些关于我的重要细节，这样你就知道起点是什么。 最重要的是

因此，这篇文章可能对需要照顾家人的人没有太大帮助。

到目前为止，我将分解我对开发人员生活的三个不同阶段的见解，因为根据情况，我的生产力和时间管理发生了很大变化。

我还想提一下，自从我的开发之旅开始以来，我一直在定期锻炼（每天至少步行 50 分钟，每周 1-2 次瑜伽），我认为这也是我的大脑能够集中注意力的一个原因比我年轻的时候好多了。

当我第一次开始学习如何编码时，在我掌握了基础知识之后很难继续专注于学习。 那时我还没有工作，所以我只能专注于编码。
但我不知道我的真正目标是什么（当然，除了在科技行业找份工作）🤷‍♀️。 所以大约 2.5 个月，我只看了 3-5 个小时的教程，感觉很迷茫。

团队项目

当我开始与其他三个开发人员一起开发一个 React 项目大约两个月时，情况发生了变化。 我终于有了一个清晰的结构，我遇到了可以以结构化方式解决的问题（在 GitHub 上），我有一个可以与之交谈的团队，我有一个可以依赖的框架（Scrum）等等。 我花在编码上的时间（我将研究和编写代码作为一个通用术语）从每天 3-5 小时增加到每天大约 10 小时📈。

这可能看起来很多，但我什至没有注意到，因为我喜欢我正在做的事情。 我经历了不同的阶段，比如一开始对 React 的更深入了解、代码审查、代码演示。 几周后，我开始使用 Figma（我在此过程中学到的东西）为应用程序创建设计，将其实现为代码，并在 Netlify 上部署应用程序。

那两个月过得真快，我的工作效率非常高，学到了很多，因为这就像一个真实的项目过程。 完成项目后，我在接下来的 3-4 周内继续保持高效，因为现在我有一些东西可以用来拉扯我的简历，所以我学会了如何写一份好的简历。 我在 GitHub 上拉皮条了项目的自述文件以及我的个人 GitHub 个人资料（自述项目） 。

在我完成所有这些“完成”之后，我再次感到有点失落，因为对于接下来要学习什么以及如何衡量它，我再次没有明确的目标。 我开始教其他初学者到目前为止我所知道的内容，并观看了有关 Instagram 克隆等主题的教程以更深入地了解 React，但我回到了每天 3-5 小时。

黑客马拉松

当我参加第一次黑客马拉松时，我的工作效率再次提高。 我有一个月的时间来创建一个项目并写下它，它再次引起了我的全神贯注——每天 10 个小时，并有一个明确的目标，即在一个月内准备好一个项目。 幸运的是我的项目是亚军🏆，所以我什至得到了我辛勤工作的奖励。

接下来的一个月，又举办了一次黑客马拉松，我又参加了。 我也开始申请工作，很快就找到了。 所以我接下来应该做什么的压力完全没有了。

当我开始工作并随后被分配到我的第一个项目时，我仍然有我想要在职业和技术知识方面实现的个人目标。

目标

我认为无论如何我都会在从事该项目时完成其中的大部分工作，但不幸的是事实并非如此。 整整五个月我几乎与 JavaScript 无关，因为我只有 bug 需要修复，所以我确实接触了 SCSS，但我自己从来没有机会编写函数或类似的东西。
由于项目已经进入最后阶段，因此我从一开始就面临时间压力，我几乎无法处理项目结构、CI/CD、架构或我们的测试。

我一点也不觉得有生产力

为了实现上述所有要点，我必须在空闲时间做几乎所有事情，这太过分了。 一周内因为压力太大，下班后太累了，周末只有2.5天。

所以这五个月主要是在工作期间处理可访问性和 git，周末只完成了一半的学习网站，两次参与开源。
在那段时间里，我几乎感觉不到生产力（尽管我每天都在做家乡最大的项目之一。疯了，对吧？😅）

然而，过去几周的好处是，我越来越多地发现自己的职业未来应该朝着哪个方向发展。 通过这个项目，我对可访问性产生了浓厚的兴趣，并为自己设定了一个目标：

我希望能够在年底称自己为可访问性和前端开发人员 (ReactJS) 。

考虑到这个目标，现在更容易划分小步骤来实现这个目标。 时间管理突然变得很自然，我的工作效率从那时起一直处于最高点。

明确的目标

优点

现在的好处是我当前的项目是一个 React 项目，而且它还处于早期阶段。 因此，我参与了完整的设置，例如测试、CI/CD、软件架构等等，而且私下不必担心太多。

整个六月我都可以利用我的工作时间来学习IAAP证书。 这也极大地促进了我的目标实现。

业余时间和爱好📚

从 6 月开始，我利用周末来获得 Google UX Design 证书，因为我也想最迟在 8 月完成它（根据 Coursera 计划，每周 5 小时需要 6 个月）。 我的结构很好，所以我想我可以做到。
我还想增加我的开源贡献，主要关注可访问性问题，因为这是我现在感到舒服和自信的领域。

即使在下班后，我仍然有精力和动力去为我的追随者开发我的学习网站、撰写博客文章等等。 为我明确定义的目标工作对我来说非常有趣和丰富，以至于我完全没有考虑其他爱好，甚至没有考虑它们。

感觉不像是工作，这是我的爱好，我的热情❤️‍🔥。

既然我男朋友也有类似的情况，那么大的承诺不会影响我们的关系，反而会丰富我们的关系，因为我们未来的计划是一样的。 我们每天一起吃午饭，并在短暂的休息时间里一起谈论任何事情。 然后在我们的假期中，我们将 100% 的注意力集中在我们身上（我们喜欢旅行，在 Covid 之前我们真的经常旅行 - 不允许使用笔记本电脑😜）。

我在学校从来都不好。 我无法专注于学习或课程。 我无法理解为什么我必须学习某些东西以及为什么要学习某些东西，并且勉强通过了学业。 这种情况多年来没有改变，这就是为什么我几乎完全是兼职工作的原因。

但当我 29 岁开始学习日语时，情况就完全不同了。 我对学习我非常喜欢的语言和文化非常感兴趣，以至于每天至少学习 3 小时汉字成为我的爱好。 这很有趣。 我很擅长。 三年后我的编程之旅也是如此。

所以我得出的结论是，如果我有一个明确的目标并且对某事充满热情，那么我在保持生产力或管理我的时间方面完全没有问题。 我花了很长时间才弄清楚这一点🥲。

感谢您的阅读和时间。 对此，我真的非常感激！

Zitouni Abdeldjallil - Zitouni Abdeldjallil - May 26 5月26日



原文:

Hi, my name is Julia, and I'm a self-taught Accessibility & Frontend Developer in my 30s who is now helping others make it into tech too.

I'm often asked by my followers how I structure my day and make sure I stay productive. Further, this topic was discussed in several Twitter spaces lately. It seems like a lot of developers struggle with these things.

So let me tell you my story.

Table Of Contents

(For simplicity, I will shorten "Time Management and Productivity" to TM&P.)

But first, let me tell you some important details about me so you know what the starting point is. The most important one is that

🙅‍♀️ I DO NOT have any real commitments, like children, a dog, or someone to take care of.
Thus, this article may not be of much help to a person who needs to take care of their family.

🎮 I DO have hobbies, like reading, Netflix, video games, and playing the piano.

👩‍❤️‍👨 I AM in a relationship for 8 years with a man who has a similar lifestyle to mine.

I'm going to break down my insights into the three different phases of my developer life so far, because depending on the situation, my productivity and time management changed quite a bit.

I would also like to mention that since the beginning of my Dev journey I have been exercising regularly (walking at least 50 minutes a day, yoga 1-2 a week), which I think is also a reason why my brain is able to concentrate much better than in my younger days.

1. TM&P on my journey to becoming a developer

When I first started learning how to code, it was pretty hard to stay focused on learning after I had mastered the basics. At that time, I didn't have a job, so I could focus solely on coding.
But I didn't know what my real goal was (other than getting a job in tech, of course) 🤷‍♀️. So for about 2.5 months, I only watched 3-5 hour tutorials and felt pretty lost.

Team project

Things changed when I started working on a React project with three other developers for about two months. I finally had a clear structure, I had issues (on GitHub) that I could work through in a structured way, I had a team to talk to, I had a framework (Scrum) that I could rely on, and more. My time spent coding (I refer to researching and writing code as a general term) went from 3-5 hours a day to about 10 hours a day 📈.

This might seem like a lot, but I didn't even notice because I liked what I was doing. I went through different phases, like a deeper understanding of React at the beginning, code reviews, code demos. After a few weeks, I started working with Figma (what I learned along the way) to create a design for the app, implement it as code, and deploy the app on Netlify.

Those two months went by really fast, and I was super productive and learned a lot because it was like a real life project process. After I finished the project, I continued to be productive for the next 3-4 weeks, because now I had something to pimp my CV with, so I learned all about how to write a good CV. I pimped the project's readme on GitHub as well as my personal GitHub profile (readme project).

After I got all that "done", I felt a bit lost again because there was again no clear goal of what to learn next and how to measure it. I started teaching other beginners what I knew so far and watched tutorials on topics like Instagram cloning to dive deeper into React, but I fell back to 3-5 hours a day.

Hackathons

My productivity increased again when I participated in my first Hackathon. I had a month to create a project and write about it, and it had my full attention again - 10 hours a day with a clear goal in mind to have a project ready in a month. And luckily my project was among the runners-up 🏆, so I even got a reward for my hard work.

The following month, another Hackathon took place and I participated in it again. I also started applying for jobs and soon found one. So the pressure of what I should do next was completely off.

2. TM&P during my first professional project at work

When I started my job and was subsequently assigned my first project, I still had personal goals that I wanted to achieve in terms of my career and technical knowledge.

Goals

🏁 I wanted to improve my knowledge of JavaScript, git, project setup (like CI/CD), architecture, testing, SCSS, accessibility.
🏁 Furthermore, I wanted to deepen my React knowledge, contribute to open source and create my learning website for my followers.

I thought I would accomplish most of these points anyway while working on the project, but unfortunately that was not the case. I had almost nothing to do with JavaScript for the whole five months, and since I only had bugs to fix, I did get in touch with SCSS, but never had the chance to write functions or anything like that myself.
Since the project was already in its final phase and I was therefore under time pressure from the beginning, I could hardly deal with the project structure, CI/CD, architecture or our test.

I didn't feel productive at all

To achieve all my points above, I would have had to do pretty much everything during my free time, and that was just too much. During the week I was too exhausted after work due to stress, and the weekend has only 2.5 days.

So I had spent those five months mostly dealing with accessibility and git during work, and only half-finished my learning website on the weekends and participated in open source twice.
I hardly felt productive during that time (even though I was working on one of the biggest projects in my hometown every day. Crazy, right? 😅)

3. TM&P during precise goal setting

The good thing about the past few weeks, however, was that I found out more and more for myself in which direction my professional future should go. Through the project, I have developed a great interest in accessibility and have set a goal for myself:

I want to be able to call myself an Accessibility & Frontend Developer (ReactJS) at the end of the year.

With this goal in mind, it was now easier to divide small steps to be able to reach this goal. Time management suddenly came naturally and my productivity has been at its highest point ever since.

Clear goals

🏁 To pass the IAAP Web Accessibility certification at the next deadline (which will be at the beginning of August). 🏁 Google UX Design Certificate (to understand accessibility at an early stage). 🏁 Deepen React knowledge

🏁 Gain knowledge in Design System, Software Architecture, Testing and much more (as I think it is important to understand accessibility in its full span and to be able to develop a project with accessibility-first approach).

Advantages

The good thing now is that my current project is a React project, plus, it is in the early stages. I am therefore involved in the complete setup, such as testing, CI/CD, software architecture and much more, and do not have to worry so much about it privately.

The whole month of June I can use my working time to study for the IAAP certificate. This also facilitates my goal achievement enormously.

Spare time and hobbies 📚

Since June I use the weekends for the Google UX Design certificate, because I also want to finish it in August at the latest (according to Coursera it is planned to take 6 months at 5h per week). I have it well structured, so I think I can do it.
I also want to increase my open source contribution and focus mainly on accessibility issues, because that's the field I feel comfortable and confident in now.

Even after work, I still have energy and motivation to work on things like my learning website for my followers, writing blog articles and much more. Working on my clearly defined goals is so much fun and enriching for me that I leave my other hobbies completely out of it, without even thinking about them.

It doesn't feel like work, it IS my hobby, my passion ❤️‍🔥.

Since my boyfriend is in a similar situation, the big commitment doesn't interfere with our relationship but actually enriches it since our future plans are the same. We have lunch together every day and spend our short breaks together talking about anything. Then on our vacations we focus 100% on us (we love to travel and before Covid we travel really often - no laptops allowed 😜).

4. Conclusion

I was never good at school. I couldn't concentrate on learning or on the lessons. I couldn't make sense of why and for what I had to learn certain things, and barely managed to pass school. This didn't change for years, which is why I worked almost exclusively part-time.

But when I started studying Japanese at the age of 29, it changed completely. I was so interested in learning the language and culture I love so much that it became my hobby to study Kanji at least 3 hours a day. It was a lot of fun. And I was good at it. Same with my journey into coding 3 years later.

So I've come to the conclusion that if I have a clear goal and am passionate about something, I have no problem at all staying productive or managing my time. And it just took me what felt like an eternity to figure that out 🥲.

Thanks for your reading and time. I really appreciate it!

Zitouni Abdeldjallil - May 26

Items.Title: 我应该编写一个新的 JavaScript 框架吗？ | Should I write a new JavaScript framework?

每周，约有 300 个速度极快、改变游戏规则的全栈 JavaScript 框架被发布到开发者生态系统中。 他们向独具慧眼的开发人员承诺全新的方法、肯定生活的开发人员体验、零配置、最大配置、所有铃声、无口哨和坠入爱河的多巴胺兴奋——一遍又一遍。

你想成为英雄吗？

现在可供公众使用的数十亿JavaScript 框架的开源维护者被奉为新时代的英雄，因为他们大胆地带领我们进入 JavaScript 的新领域——甚至更远。

你可能会问自己——“我应该写一个新的 JavaScript 框架吗？”

这个问题的答案远远超出了本文的范围。 相反，我建立了一个网站。 本网站将带您踏上一段鼓舞人心的旅程，了解构建新 JavaScript 框架时要考虑的基本概念，并让您有信心最终mkdir bestframework.js && cd bestframework.js && git init 。

原文:

Each week, around 300 blazing-fast, game-changing, full-stack JavaScript frameworks are released into the developer ecosystem. They promise the discerning developer radical new approaches, life-affirming developer experiences, zero-config, maximum-config, all-bells, no-whistles, and the dopamine high of falling in love — over and over and over again.

Do you want to be a hero?

Open source maintainers of the billions of JavaScript frameworks now available for public consumption are worshipped as heroes of a new era, as they lead us boldly into a new landscape of JavaScript — and beyond.

And you're probably asking yourself — "Should I write a new JavaScript framework?"

What's the answer?

The answer to this question is far beyond the scope of this post. Instead, I built a website. This website takes you on an inspiring journey through the essential concepts to consider when building your new JavaScript framework, and gives you the confidence to finally mkdir bestframework.js && cd bestframework.js && git init.

Start your journey here.

Items.Title: 如何建立数据科学组合 | How to Build a Data Science Portfolio
Items.Description:
早些时候，招聘是基于我们在简历或简历中提到的技术知识。 今天的组织需要更多与行业相关的实践经验，而不是理论知识。 雇主会寻找候选人在该特定领域所做的工作。 因此，整体投资组合将有助于为选择候选人设定基准。 嗯，这不是每个 IT 领域都需要的，但是如果我们考虑数据科学行业，那么拥有一个投资组合是必不可少的，因为雇主会根据你的工作和你所拥有的实践知识来决定。

拥有数据科学作品集将帮助您展示使您在出现相同工作的其他候选人中脱颖而出的技能。 如果您正确地完成了作业，它将帮助您获得理想的工作——您所要做的就是在投资组合中展示您的数据科学。 事不宜迟，让我们了解拥有数据科学组合对于作为数据分析师、数据科学家或任何其他与数据相关的工作角色获得顶级职位的重要性。

什么是数据科学组合？
与任何其他专业作品集一样，数据科学作品集可以帮助候选人通过他们的项目展示他们的才能和技能。 拥有扎实的作品集将突出您解决研究问题、分析数据、收集项目见解的能力、与其他团队成员合作的能力以及与用户交流您的发现的能力。 只有拥有扎实的技术背景，这一切才有可能。

拥有数据科学作品集将有助于与您的招聘经理建立信任，因此他将确保您是该工作的理想候选人。 此外，如果您是该行业的初学者并且没有任何技术领域的经验，您也不必担心。 有吸引力的数据科学作品集会将招聘人员的注意力更多地转移到您的作品集上，以判断您的技能。

对于初学者来说，为数据科学创建投资组合很容易，因为它提供了可以在项目中使用的各种公共数据集。 如果您有创造力，您甚至可以使用公共数据集来适应每个功能，而不是花在项目上。

为什么需要有一个数据科学组合？
好吧，大多数雇主都在寻找您拥有的学位和技术技能。 他们主要关注你从哪里获得学位。 如果它是一所著名的大学或研究所，他们会相信候选人必须具备数据科学工作所需的正确技能。 但是，如果您没有适当的数据科学学位，那么您获得这份工作的机会就最低。 但是，如果您有一个展示您的创造力、才能和技术技能的数据科学作品集，那么您将被考虑担任这份工作。 作为一名数据科学家，需要强大的技术知识以及如何使用各种工具来处理数据。

因此，随身携带数据科学组合以获得您想到的理想工作非常重要。 如果您是数据科学的新手，并且不知道如何开始处理您的数据科学组合，那么您可以通过下面提到的技巧来创建一个并在面试中引起注意。
创建数据科学组合的技巧

  • 创建 GitHub 个人资料
    使用 GitHub，您将能够托管您的数据科学项目的远程版本，以便所有人都能看到。 确保您有一个活跃的 GitHub 个人资料，以便您可以将链接添加到您的简历。 活跃的个人资料是指您定期对其进行处理以进行适当的更改并且对您的观众可见。 为您的个人资料准备一个 readme.md 以自定义您的主页是一个很好的做法。

  • 正确使用 Kaggle
    如果您想定期练习和展示您的技能，那么建议您拥有一个 Kaggle 帐户。 该网站在几家大公司中很受欢迎，并用于开展招聘合适的数据科学候选人的竞赛。 您可以通过它的测试并获得对您有益的奖牌，并在面试中加分。 此外，您可以在您的简历中添加您的 Kaggle 帐户的链接。 下面是 Kaggle 配置文件的示例。

  • 参加各种比赛和黑客马拉松
    如果您想测试您在数据科学方面的技术知识，那么您可以参加各种在线比赛和黑客马拉松。 它将帮助您了解您的立场以及您在同行群体中的位置。 您甚至可以在简历中添加您的成就以及该比赛的在线证书链接。 您可以通过各种在线平台参加考试并测试您的技能。

  • 使用 HackerRank 做好准备
    要成为一名数据科学家，您需要具备各种技术技能和对 Python 等编程语言的深入了解。 您可以通过参与各种平台来提高自己的技能，从而为自己做好准备。 您可以解决和练习 HackerRank 网站上提供的问题，并将您的成就添加到您的简历中，以便成为个人资料的更好候选人。

拥有数据科学家组合的主要目的是展示你的技术技能并让招聘经理相信你是这份工作的合适人选。 因此，请确保将案例研究添加到您的个人资料中。 我们已经提到了一些您可以在简历中创建和提及的项目类型。

  • 数据清洗项目
    数据科学家的关键角色之一是清理和整理任何公司的杂乱数据。 因此，最好添加一个涉及数据清理的项目。 确保使用现有的混乱数据集，然后尝试探索数据以清理它并执行分析以获得更好的见解。 这将为您的选择奠定坚实的基础。

  • 数据讲故事项目
    由于任何公司的数据都在膨胀，有大量的原始数据没有被整理，没有任何意义。 作为一名数据科学家，你的工作是找到现有数据中的相关性并将它们融入故事叙述中，以便它们有意义。 您可以添加一个已实现此方法的示例。

  • 机器学习项目
    如果您包含机器学习项目，它将有助于指定您在所需技术知识方面的进展。 创建机器学习项目需要了解可以处理输入数据并创建输出的算法。 您可以展示您对算法的理解，这对招聘经理来说是一个加分项，并让您担任技术角色。

结论
数据科学家的需求量很大，需要独特而强大的技能才能成为其中一员。 如果您不知道如何在项目中实施这些知识，那么拥有正确的知识还不足以被选中。 因此，您必须专注于实践知识，而不是拥有理论知识，因为招聘人员关注候选人如何应用这些知识。 因此，拥有一个投资组合将对候选人和招聘人员都有帮助。 候选人将能够展示他们的才能，招聘人员将获得根据候选人的才能选择候选人的基础。 因此，请务必创建一个数据科学家组合，以获得更好的工作机会。



原文:

Earlier, recruitments were based on the technical knowledge that we mentioned in the CV or resume. Today organizations demand more industry-relevant hands-on experience over theoretical knowledge. Employers look for the work the candidate has done in that particular field. Thus an overall portfolio will help in setting a benchmark for selecting the candidates. Well, this is not required for every IT field, but if we consider the Data Science industry, then having a portfolio is essential as the employers will decide based on your work and the practical knowledge you have.

Having a Data Science portfolio will help you showcase the skills that will allow you to stand out among other candidates appearing for the same job. If you have done your homework correctly, it will help you get the dream job you desired—all you have to do is showcase your data science on the portfolio. Without much ado, let’s understand the importance of having a data science portfolio to land a top gig as a data analyst, data scientist, or any other data-related job role.

What is a Data Science portfolio?
Like any other professional portfolio, a data science portfolio helps candidate showcase their talent and skills through their projects. Having a solid portfolio will highlight your ability to solve research questions, analyze data, gather project insights, how well you can collaborate with other team members, and your ability to communicate your findings to the users. All this is possible only if you have a solid technical background.

Having a data science portfolio will help establish trust with your hiring manager so he will be sure that you are the desired candidate for the job. Also, you do not have to worry if you are a beginner in the industry and do not possess any experience in any technical field. An attractive data science portfolio will divert the recruiter’s attention more towards your portfolio to judge your skills.

Creating a portfolio for data science is easy for beginners as it offers various public datasets that they can use in their projects. If you have creativity, you can even accommodate every feature using the public dataset rather than spending on the project.

Why is there a need to have a data science portfolio?
Well, most employers look for the degree and technical skills that you possess. They majorly focus on where you got your degree from. If it is a prestigious college or institute, they will be confident that the candidate must have the right skill set that is required for the data science job. But if you do not have a proper degree in data science, you have the lowest chance of getting the job. But if you have a data science portfolio showcasing your creativity, talent, and technical skills, you will be considered for the job. Being a data scientist will require strong technical knowledge and how to use various tools for working with data.

Thus, it is important that you carry your data science portfolio to get the dream job you thought of. If you are new to data science and do not have any idea how you can start working on your data science portfolio then you can go through the below-mentioned tips for creating one and get noticed during interviews.
Tips to Create a Data Science Portfolio

  • Create a GitHub Profile
    With GitHub, you will be able to host the remote version of your data science project to be visible to everyone. Make sure you have an active GitHub profile so that you can add a link to your CV. an active profile refers that you are working on it regularly to make adequate changes and are visible to your viewers. It is a good practice to have a readme.md for your profile for customizing your homepage.

  • Make proper use of Kaggle
    If you want to practice and showcase your skills on a regular basis, then it is recommended to have a Kaggle account. This site is popular among several big companies and used for carrying out competitions for hiring suitable Data science candidates. Kaggle also helps in understanding the techniques and tips that you can use while working with data. You can go through its tests and earn medals that will be beneficial for you and add a plus point during the interview. Also, you can add the link for your Kaggle account on your CV. below is an example of the Kaggle profile.

  • Participate in various Competitions and Hackathons
    If you want to test your technical knowledge in data science then you can appear for various online competitions and hackathons. It will help you to know where you stand and your position among the peer groups. You can even add your achievements in your CV along with a link to your online certificate for that competition. There are various online platforms where you can appear for the exam and test your skills.

  • Prepare yourself using HackerRank
    For becoming a data scientist, you need to have various technical skills and strong knowledge of programming languages like Python. You can prepare yourself by engaging yourself in various platforms for enhancing your skills. You can solve and practice questions available on the HackerRank website and add your achievements to your CV for being a better candidate for the profile.

*Some examples for the Data science portfolio *
The main purpose of having a Data Scientist portfolio is to showcase your technical skills and convince the hiring manager that you are the right candidate for the job. So make sure, you add the case studies to your profile. We have mentioned some of the project types that you can create and mention in your CV.

  • Data cleaning projects
    One of the key roles of a data scientist is to clean and arrange the messy data of any company. So it is a better idea to add a project that involves data cleaning. Make sure to use existing messy data sets, then try to explore data to clean it up and perform analysis to get better insights. This will create a strong base for you to get selected.

  • Data Storytelling Project
    Due to the expansion in data for any company, there is a huge amount of raw data that is not arranged and does not make any sense. Being a data scientist, it is your job to find the correlation in the existing data and to fit them into a story narration so they make some sense. You can add an example where you have implemented this approach.

  • Machine Learning Project
    If you include a machine learning project, it will help in specifying your progress in the required technical knowledge. Creating a machine learning project will require knowledge of algorithms that will work on the entered data and create an output. You can showcase your understanding of the algorithms that is a plus point for the hiring manager and put you in the technical role.

Conclusion
Data scientists are in great demand and require a unique and strong skill set to become one. Having the right knowledge is not enough to get selected if you do not know how to implement that knowledge in a project. So instead of having theoretical knowledge, you must focus on practical one as recruiters focus on how a candidate is able to apply that knowledge. So having a portfolio will help both the candidate and the recruiter. Candidates will be able to showcase their talents and the recruiter will get a base for selecting the candidate based on the candidate’s talent. So make sure to create a Data scientist portfolio for better job opportunities.

Items.Title: 清洁架构：层和边界 | Clean Architecture: Layers and Boundaries
Items.Description:
在了解了Clean Architecture的关键概念以及围绕它的一些重要概念（如SOLID和组件原理）之后，就更容易理解它的分层及其边界。

一个系统可以由三个基本组件定义：UI、业务规则和数据库——在更大的系统中还有更多的组件。 为了解释层和边界的概念，将使用 MARTIN (2017) 制作的示例，该示例描述了 1970 年代称为 Hunt the Wumpus 的游戏。

游戏规则与此示例无关，因为重点将放在实现本身上。

考虑到使用基于文本的 UI，需要将游戏规则解耦，使其可以有不同的语言。 无论语言如何，规则都将使用 API 与 UI 进行通信，并且 UI 将转换为请求的语言。 这样，所有 UI 组件都将能够使用相同的游戏规则。 假设游戏状态保存在持久存储中，则游戏规则不需要知道细节，因此将创建一个 API 来将这些规则与数据存储组件进行通信。

游戏规则也必须不知道不同类型的数据存储，因此必须按照依赖规则正确处理依赖关系，如游戏的基本结构图所示（来源：马丁（2017））。

在这个简短的描述和基于清洁架构概念的基础上，可以想象清洁架构将很容易地应用在这个例子中。 然而，并不是所有的架构边界都是清晰的。

在这个例子中，语言不是唯一的 UI 变化轴，通信机制也可以多种多样，例如聊天应用程序、普通窗口或文本消息。 有无数的变化可能性，这表明这些变化轴定义了一个架构限制。 因此，有必要创建一个 API 来跨越这个限制，并将语言与通信机制隔离开来。

上图反映了重新制定的游戏结构（来源：MARTIN (2017)），虚线矩形是 API，写成“游戏规则”的矩形包含游戏规则。 游戏规则组件位于图表顶部，因为它包含最高级别的策略。 该组织划分数据流，左侧与用户通信，右侧用于数据持久化。 随着系统的发展，组件结构可以划分为具有不同层次结构的多个流，这使得架构变得越来越复杂。

结论

这个简单的例子是为了表明架构边界无处不在，这取决于软件架构师来识别需求。

还必须知道，实施这些边界是昂贵的，但同时，如果不在开发早期实施，随着时间的推移，它们可能会产生更大的成本。

马丁强调，这些决定不是一次做出的，有必要观察系统的演变，分析哪些地方可能需要边界，以及如果它们没有明确定义的边界会发生什么。 他还强调了将实施这些边界的成本与忽略它们的成本进行比较的重要性，始终寻求在实施成本低于忽略它们的成本的最佳位置实施正确的边界。

参考

  • 第一个。 编。 美国：Prentice Hall 出版社，2017 年。ISBN 0134494164。


原文:

After understanding about the key concepts of Clean Architecture and some important concepts that surround it such as SOLID and component principles, it is easier to understand about its division into layers and its boundaries.

A system can be defined by three basic components: the UI, the business rules and the database - and in larger systems there are many more components. To explain the concept of layers and boundaries, the example made by MARTIN (2017) will be used, which describes the game of the 1970s called Hunt the Wumpus.

The rules of the game are irrelevant for this example, as the focus will be on the implementation itself.

Considering that a text-based UI is used, it is necessary to decouple the game rules so it can have different languages. The rules will communicate with the UI using an API, regardless of language, and the UI will translate to the requested language. In this way all UI components will be able to make use of the same rules of the game. Assuming that the game state is kept in a persistent store, there is no need for the game rules to know the details, so an API will be created to communicate these rules with the data store component.

The game rules must also not know about the different types of data storage, so the dependencies must be correctly addressed following the Dependency Rule, as seen in the game's basic structure figure (source: MARTIN (2017)).

After this brief description and based on Clean Architecture concepts, it is imagined that clean architecture would be easily applied in this example. However, not all architectural boundaries are clear.

In this example, the language is not the only UI change axis, the communication mechanism can also be varied, such as a chat application, a normal window or text messages. There are countless possibilities for change, which shows that there is an architectural limit defined by these axes of change. Therefore, it is necessary to create an API to cross this limit and isolate the language from the communication mechanism.

The figure above reflects the reformulated game structure (source: MARTIN (2017)), the dotted rectangles are the APIs and the rectangle written "Game Rules" contains the game rules. The Rules of the Game component is positioned at the top of the diagram, as it contains the highest level policies. This organization divides the data flow, being the left side to communicate with the user and the right side for data persistence. As systems grow, the component structure can be divided into several flows with different hierarchies, which makes the architecture increasingly complex.

MARTIN (2017) concludes this issue by stating that the role of the software architect is to assess costs, determine architectural limits and which of these limits should be fully implemented, which should be partially implemented and which should be ignored.

Conclusion

This simple example is meant to show that there are architectural boundaries everywhere, and it's up to software architects to recognize the needs.

It is also necessary to know that implementing these boundaries is something expensive, but at the same time, if not implemented early in development, they can have even greater costs over time.

Martin reinforces that these decisions are not made just once, that it is necessary to observe the evolution of the system, analyze where boundaries may be necessary and what can happen if they do not have well-defined boundaries. He also reinforces the importance of comparing the costs of implementing these boundaries with the cost of ignoring them, always looking to implement the right boundaries at the sweet spot where the cost of implementation is less than the cost of ignoring them.

References

  • MARTIN, Robert C. Clean Architecture: A Craftsman’s Guide to Software Structure and Design. 1st. ed. USA: Prentice Hall Press, 2017. ISBN 0134494164.

Items.Title: 编排传奇模式实现 | Choreography Saga Pattern Implementation
Items.Description:
通过维护应用程序级别的锁定机制，可以应用 Saga 模式来执行跨多个微服务的分布式事务。

在传统的单体应用中，单个数据库执行所有应用程序范围的事务，因此它可以使用表/行级锁定来强制执行ACID 合规性。 基于微服务的系统通常每个服务都有一个数据库，因此这种 ACID 合规性无法在数据库层实现。

就像芭蕾舞演员如何根据音乐的声音相互协调地执行一组精确的步骤一样，参与事务的每个微服务都会发出其他微服务可以捕获并采取相应行动的事件。 例如：

  • 用户通过网站提交订单
  • 库存微服务可以对事件采取行动并调整库存。
  • 当INVENTORY_RESERVED发出时，交付微服务可以安排交付
  • 最后订单创建的网站将向用户显示交货详细信息。

但是，当库存微服务仅仅因为不可用而拒绝或失败保留特定项目的请求时会发生什么？ 由于帐户微服务已经从用户帐户中扣除了金额，并且数据库级别的回滚为时已晚，因此必须在帐户微服务中执行补偿事务。 编排最适合事件驱动的通信

在管弦乐队中，指挥是一位音乐专业人士，他用双手的动作指挥表演。 指挥通过向其他音乐家提供指导来设定节奏并统一管弦乐队。

执行指挥者收到的指令/命令的音乐家是参与分布式事务的微服务。 与微服务作用于其他微服务事件的编排模式不同，基于 Orchestrator 的 Sagas 需要一个称为Saga Orchestrator的单独模块/服务来控制事务流。

一旦向 Saga 编排器发布了启动分布式事务的请求，它就会以预定义的顺序命令每个微服务执行与事务相关的一些任务。 微服务执行它们的任务并将结果通常发布到单个回复通道中。 根据回复通道中收集的事件，编排器可以决定是继续事务还是在相应的微服务上执行补偿事务。

让我们将上述排序系统转换为 Orchestrator 模式：

  • 用户通过网站提交订单并发送请求/命令以启动Order Saga Orchestrator(OSO)
  • 如果账户有足够的资金，Accounts 微服务将 PAYEMENT_RECEIVED事件发送到回复通道。
  • 如果库存可用，它将向回复通道发出INVENTORY_RESERVED事件。
  • 但是，如果 OSO 收到INVENTORY_REJECTED ，它将命令 Accounts 微服务执行补偿交易（偿还扣除的金额）

实现编舞传奇

我们将使用 Spring Boot 项目实现上述场景。 我们将使用三个队列和一个Exchange 。 消息生产者将消息发送到交换器，交换器将消息路由到队列，以便消费者可以使用它们。

交换机如何知道目标队列？ 好吧，每条消息都有一个嵌入在标头中的称为路由键的属性。 创建队列时，它将通过路由键绑定到交换器，以便交换器知道给定消息应该在哪里结束。

一张图片胜过千言万语：

当使用路由键绑定到许多队列时，交换被称为主题交换。 这种关系在图中突出显示的黄色框中进行了描述。

项目设置概述

我们将为每个微服务创建三个 Spring Boot 项目：

  • 订购服务
    • 创建新订单
    • 监听订单状态更新
  • 账户服务
    • 检查账户是否有足够的资金
    • 成功借记账户
    • 补偿交易请求的信用账户
    • 发布订单状态失败
  • 库存服务
    • 检查请求的库存项目是否可用
    • 成功调整库存
    • 请求帐户服务以补偿交易
    • 发布状态失败

依赖项

<!-- All microservices' pom.xml --> <dependency> <groupId> org.springframework.boot </groupId> <artifactId> spring-boot-starter-amqp </artifactId> </dependency> ... <dependencyManagement> <dependencies> <dependency> <groupId> org.springframework.cloud </groupId> <artifactId> spring-cloud-dependencies </artifactId> <version> ${spring-cloud.version} </version> <type> pom </type> <scope> import </scope> </dependency> </dependencies> </dependencyManagement>

配置 RabbitMQ

所有微服务都声明队列和绑定，以便队列和交换随时可用，而不管微服务的启动顺序如何。 检查代码注释以获得更好的理解。

 @Configuration public class RabbitBeanConfig { public static final String SINGLE_ORDER_TOPIC_EXCH_NAME = "single-orders-exchange" ; public static final String SINGLE_ORDER_QUEUE_NAME = "single-order-queue" ; public static final String SINGLE_ORDER_ROUTING_KEY = "orders.create.*" ; @Bean Queue singleOrderqueue () { return new Queue ( SINGLE_ORDER_QUEUE_NAME , false ); // New orders received at our Restful endpoint will be placed on this queue to be consumed by Accounts microservice } @Bean Queue orderUpdateQueue () { return new Queue ( "order-update" , false ); // Stores the final order outcome - Order Manager microservice will consume this } @Bean Queue paymentQueue () { return new Queue ( "payment" , false ); // Successful order payments end up here to be consumed by Inventory service } @Bean TopicExchange singleOrderExchange () { return new TopicExchange ( SINGLE_ORDER_TOPIC_EXCH_NAME ); //Single Topic Exchange to route messages to queues using a Routing key } @Bean Binding singleOrderBinding ( Queue singleOrderqueue , TopicExchange singleOrderExchange ) { return BindingBuilder . bind ( singleOrderqueue ). to ( singleOrderExchange ). with ( SINGLE_ORDER_ROUTING_KEY ); // Bind each queue to the Exchange } @Bean Binding orderUpdateBinding ( Queue orderUpdateQueue , TopicExchange singleOrderExchange ) { return BindingBuilder . bind ( orderUpdateQueue ). to ( singleOrderExchange ). with ( "orders.update" ); } @Bean Binding inventoryBinding ( Queue paymentQueue , TopicExchange singleOrderExchange ) { return BindingBuilder . bind ( paymentQueue ). to ( singleOrderExchange ). with ( "orders.pay" ); } @Bean MessageConverter messageConverter () { // Encode/Decode Java objects to/from JSON return new Jackson2JsonMessageConverter (); } @Bean RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate ( final ConnectionFactory connectionFactory , final MessageConverter messageConverter ) { final RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate = new RabbitTemplate (); rabbitTemplate . setExchange ( SINGLE_ORDER_TOPIC_EXCH_NAME ); //Otherwise default rabbit queue rabbitTemplate . setConnectionFactory ( connectionFactory ); rabbitTemplate . setMessageConverter ( messageConverter ); // Set the message converter to the template return rabbitTemplate ; } }

队列监听器

订单管理器需要监听包含交易最终状态的order-update队列。

 @Component public class OrderUpdateListener { @Autowired Queue orderUpdateQueue ; @RabbitListener ( queues = "#{orderUpdateQueue.getName()}" ) public void onOrderUpdate ( SingleOrder order ) { System . out . println ( "ORDER_SERVICE|" + order . id + "|ORDER STATUS UPDATED: " + order ); } }

创建新订单时，帐户服务应检查是否有足够的资金进行交易。 当库存服务拒绝交易时，它还应该支持补偿交易。

 @Component public class OrderCreatedListener { @Autowired Queue createOrderQueue ; @Autowired RabbitTemplate rabbit ; @RabbitListener ( queues = "#{createOrderQueue.getName()}" ) public void onOrderCreated ( SingleOrder order , @Header ( AmqpHeaders . RECEIVED_ROUTING_KEY ) String key ) { System . out . println ( "ACCOUNT_SERVICE|" + order . id + "|RESPONDING TO ORDER CREATED key: " + key ); String [] tokens = key . split ( "\\." ); if ( tokens . length > 1 && tokens [ tokens . length - 1 ]. equals ( "failed" )) { //Compensating transaction System . out . println ( "ACCOUNT_SERVICE|" + order . id + "|COMPENSATING AMOUNT = +" + order . cost ); } else { if ( order . cost > 100 ) { System . out . println ( "ACCOUNT_SERVICE|" + order . id + "|NOT ENOUGH FUNDS" ); order . isFailed = true ; order . failureReason = "NOT ENOUGH FUNDS: " + order . cost ; rabbit . convertAndSend ( "orders.update" , order ); } else { System . out . println ( "ACCOUNT_SERVICE|" + order . id + "|DEBITED AMOUNT = -" + order . cost ); rabbit . convertAndSend ( "orders.pay" , order ); } } } }

同样，Inventory 服务会侦听经过验证的付款队列以接受/拒绝交易。 如果该项目不可用，它应该向order-update队列发送一条消息，以便订单管理器可以向客户显示拒绝的订单详细信息。 它还应该通知客户经理运行补偿交易。

 @Component public class PaymentReceivedListener { @Autowired Queue paymentQueue ; @Autowired RabbitTemplate template ; @RabbitListener ( queues = "#{paymentQueue.getName()}" ) public void reserveInventory ( SingleOrder order ) { System . out . println ( "INVENTORY_SERVICE|" + order . id + "|VERIFIED PAYMENT" ); if ( order . qty > 5 ) { System . out . println ( "INVENTORY_SERVICE|" + order . id + "|STOCKS NOT AVAILABLE" ); order . isFailed = true ; order . failureReason = "STOCKS NOT AVAILABLE: " + order . qty ; template . convertAndSend ( "orders.create.failed" , order ); template . convertAndSend ( "orders.update" , order ); } else { System . out . println ( "INVENTORY_SERVICE|" + order . id + "|STOCKS RESERVED: QTY" + order . qty ); template . convertAndSend ( "orders.update" , order ); } } }

我们快完成了。 我们的简单订单创建 RESTful Web 端点如下所示。

 @RestController @RequestMapping ( "/single-order" ) public class SingleOrderResource { @Autowired RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate ; @GetMapping () public SingleOrder createSingleOrder ( @RequestParam String item , @RequestParam int qty ) { int id = ThreadLocalRandom . current (). nextInt ( 100 , 500 + 1 ); SingleOrder order = new SingleOrder ( id , item , qty ); System . out . println ( "ORDER_SERVICE|NEW ORDER:" + order ); rabbitTemplate . convertAndSend ( "orders.create.new" , order ); return order ; } }

这几乎就是您将如何实现基于 Saga 的编排 :) 如果您有任何问题，请在评论部分告诉我。



原文:

Saga pattern can be applied to perform distributed transactions spanning across multiple microservices by maintaining an application level locking mechanism.

In a traditional monolith, a single database performs all the application wide transactions so it can use table/row level locking to enforce ACID compliance. Microservices based systems usually have a database per service so that this ACID compliance could not be achieved in the database layer.

Saga pattern can be implemented in two ways:

1. Choreography/Event based Saga

Much like how ballet dancers perform set of precise steps in coordination with each other to the sound of music, each microservice participate in the transaction emits events that other microservices can capture and act accordingly. For eg:

  • A user submits an order through a website
  • NEW_ORDER event will be created.
  • Accounts microservice will deduct the order amount from the customer account and emit PAYEMENT_RECEIVED event.
  • Inventory microservice can act on the event and adjust the inventory.
  • When the INVENTORY_RESERVED emitted, the delivery microservice can schedule the delivery
  • Finally order created website will display the delivery details to the user.

But what happens when the inventory microservice rejects or fails the request to reserve a particular item simply because it is not available? Since the account microservice has already deducted the amount from the user's account and it's too late for a database level rollback, a compensating transaction has to be performed in the Accounts microservice. (repayment of the deducted amount) Again such error/failure scenarios are communicated to others by the same event emitting pattern.

Choreography best fits in event-driven communication

2. Orchestrator based Saga

In a musical orchestra, a conductor is a musical professional who directs the performance with movements of hands. Conductor sets the tempo and unifies the orchestra by providing instructions to the fellow musicians.

Orchestrator service in the Saga pattern acts as the conductor. The musicians who act on the instructions/commands received by the conductor are the microservices participate in the distributed transaction. Unlike choreography pattern where microservices act upon other microservices' events, Orchestrator based Sagas require a separate module/service known as the Saga Orchestrator to control the flow of the transaction.

Once a request has been posted to initiate a distributed transaction to the Saga orchestrator, it commands each microservice in a predefined sequence to do some task related to the transaction. Microservices perform their tasks and publish results typically into a single reply channel. Based on the events gathered in the reply channel, the orchestrator can decide whether to continue the transaction or execute compensating transactions on the corresponding microservices.

Let's translate the above ordering system to Orchestrator pattern:

  • A user submits an order through a website and sends a request/command to initiate Order Saga Orchestrator(OSO)
  • OSO commands Accounts microservice to deduct the order amount from the customer account
  • If the account has enough funds, Accounts microservice emits PAYEMENT_RECEIVED event into the reply channel.
  • OSO commands Inventory microservice to reserve inventory.
  • If the inventory is available it will emit INVENTORY_RESERVED event into the reply channel.
  • If however OSO receives an INVENTORY_REJECTED, it will command the Accounts microservice to execute compensating transaction (repayment of the deducted amount)

Orchestrator best fits in command-driven communication

Implementing Choreography Saga

We will be implementing the above discussed scenario with a Spring Boot project. RabbitMQ's Topic Exchange will be utilized for routing messages for multiple queues via a routing key.

A quick refresher on RabbitMQ

RabbitMQ is an opensource message broker which allows storing messages in queues. We will be using three queues and an Exchange. Message producers send messages to exchanges and exchanges route messages to queues so that they can be consumed by consumers.

How does the exchange knows the destination queue? Well each message has a property called a routing key embedded in the header. When a queue is created, it will be bound to the exchange by the routing key so that exchange knows where a given message should end up.

A picture is worth a thousand words:

An exchange is known as a Topic Exchange when it's bound to many queues using a routing key. This relationship is depicted in the highlighted yellow boxes in the diagram.

Project setup overview

We will be creating three Spring Boot projects for each microservice:

  • Order Service
    • Creates a new order
    • Listens to status updates of the order
  • Accounts Service
    • Checks if the account has enough funds
    • Debits account on success
    • Credits account on compensating transaction request
    • Publishes order status failure
  • Inventory Service
    • Checks if inventory item requested is available
    • Adjust inventory on success
    • Requests account service for compensating transaction
    • Publishes status failure

Dependencies 

<!-- All microservices' pom.xml -->
<dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-amqp</artifactId>
</dependency> ... <dependencyManagement> <dependencies> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId> <artifactId>spring-cloud-dependencies</artifactId> <version>${spring-cloud.version}</version> <type>pom</type> <scope>import</scope> </dependency> </dependencies>
</dependencyManagement>

Configuring RabbitMQ

RabbitMQ Beans definition

All Microservices declare queues and bindings so that queues and exchanges readily available regardless of the microservices' start order. Check code comments to get a better understanding.

@Configuration
public class RabbitBeanConfig { public static final String SINGLE_ORDER_TOPIC_EXCH_NAME = "single-orders-exchange"; public static final String SINGLE_ORDER_QUEUE_NAME = "single-order-queue"; public static final String SINGLE_ORDER_ROUTING_KEY = "orders.create.*"; @Bean Queue singleOrderqueue() { return new Queue(SINGLE_ORDER_QUEUE_NAME, false); // New orders received at our Restful endpoint will be placed on this queue to be consumed by Accounts microservice } @Bean Queue orderUpdateQueue() { return new Queue("order-update", false); // Stores the final order outcome - Order Manager microservice will consume this } @Bean Queue paymentQueue() { return new Queue("payment", false); // Successful order payments end up here to be consumed by Inventory service } @Bean TopicExchange singleOrderExchange() { return new TopicExchange(SINGLE_ORDER_TOPIC_EXCH_NAME); //Single Topic Exchange to route messages to queues using a Routing key } @Bean Binding singleOrderBinding(Queue singleOrderqueue, TopicExchange singleOrderExchange) { return BindingBuilder.bind(singleOrderqueue).to(singleOrderExchange).with(SINGLE_ORDER_ROUTING_KEY); // Bind each queue to the Exchange } @Bean Binding orderUpdateBinding(Queue orderUpdateQueue, TopicExchange singleOrderExchange) { return BindingBuilder.bind(orderUpdateQueue).to(singleOrderExchange).with("orders.update"); } @Bean Binding inventoryBinding(Queue paymentQueue, TopicExchange singleOrderExchange) { return BindingBuilder.bind(paymentQueue).to(singleOrderExchange).with("orders.pay"); } @Bean MessageConverter messageConverter() { // Encode/Decode Java objects to/from JSON  return new Jackson2JsonMessageConverter(); } @Bean RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate(final ConnectionFactory connectionFactory, final MessageConverter messageConverter) { final RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate = new RabbitTemplate(); rabbitTemplate.setExchange(SINGLE_ORDER_TOPIC_EXCH_NAME); //Otherwise default rabbit queue rabbitTemplate.setConnectionFactory(connectionFactory); rabbitTemplate.setMessageConverter(messageConverter); // Set the message converter to the template return rabbitTemplate; } }

Queue listeners

Order Manager needs to listen to order-update queue in which contains the final status of the transaction.

@Component
public class OrderUpdateListener { @Autowired Queue orderUpdateQueue; @RabbitListener(queues = "#{orderUpdateQueue.getName()}") public void onOrderUpdate(SingleOrder order) { System.out.println("ORDER_SERVICE|"+order.id+"|ORDER STATUS UPDATED: "+order); }
}

SingleOrder is a simple DTO object representing Saga transaction details.

public class SingleOrder implements Serializable { public int id; public String item; public int qty; public int cost; private int costFactor = 10; public boolean isFailed; public String failureReason; public SingleOrder() { } public SingleOrder(int id, String item, int qty) { this.id = id; this.item = item; this.qty = qty; this.cost = qty * costFactor; }
}

Accounts service and the Inventory service has listeners configured as well. When a new Order is created Accounts service should check if there are enough funds to do the transaction. It should also support compensating transactions when Inventory service rejects the transaction.

@Component
public class OrderCreatedListener { @Autowired Queue createOrderQueue; @Autowired RabbitTemplate rabbit; @RabbitListener(queues = "#{createOrderQueue.getName()}") public void onOrderCreated(SingleOrder order, @Header(AmqpHeaders.RECEIVED_ROUTING_KEY) String key) { System.out.println("ACCOUNT_SERVICE|"+order.id+"|RESPONDING TO ORDER CREATED key: "+key); String[] tokens = key.split("\\."); if (tokens.length > 1 && tokens[tokens.length - 1].equals("failed")) { //Compensating transaction System.out.println("ACCOUNT_SERVICE|"+order.id+"|COMPENSATING AMOUNT = +"+order.cost); } else { if (order.cost > 100) { System.out.println("ACCOUNT_SERVICE|"+order.id+"|NOT ENOUGH FUNDS"); order.isFailed = true; order.failureReason = "NOT ENOUGH FUNDS: " + order.cost; rabbit.convertAndSend("orders.update", order); } else { System.out.println("ACCOUNT_SERVICE|"+order.id+"|DEBITED AMOUNT = -"+order.cost); rabbit.convertAndSend("orders.pay", order); } } }
}

Similarly the Inventory service listens to the verified payments queue for accepting/rejecting the transaction. If the item is not available it should send a message to order-update queue so that the Order Manager can show rejected order details to the customer. It should also notify Account Manager to run a compensating transaction.

@Component
public class PaymentReceivedListener { @Autowired Queue paymentQueue; @Autowired RabbitTemplate template; @RabbitListener(queues = "#{paymentQueue.getName()}") public void reserveInventory(SingleOrder order) { System.out.println("INVENTORY_SERVICE|"+order.id+"|VERIFIED PAYMENT"); if (order.qty > 5) { System.out.println("INVENTORY_SERVICE|"+order.id+"|STOCKS NOT AVAILABLE"); order.isFailed = true; order.failureReason = "STOCKS NOT AVAILABLE: " + order.qty; template.convertAndSend("orders.create.failed", order); template.convertAndSend("orders.update", order); } else { System.out.println("INVENTORY_SERVICE|"+order.id+"|STOCKS RESERVED: QTY" + order.qty); template.convertAndSend("orders.update", order); } }
}

We are almost done. Our simple order creation restful web endpoint would look like as follows.

@RestController
@RequestMapping("/single-order")
public class SingleOrderResource { @Autowired RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate; @GetMapping() public SingleOrder createSingleOrder(@RequestParam String item, @RequestParam int qty) { int id = ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextInt(100, 500 + 1); SingleOrder order = new SingleOrder(id, item, qty); System.out.println("ORDER_SERVICE|NEW ORDER:"+order); rabbitTemplate.convertAndSend("orders.create.new", order); return order; }
}

That's pretty much how you would implement a choreography based Saga :) If you have any questions please do let me know in the comment's section.

Items.Title: 如何开发玩赚钱 (P2E) 游戏？ | How to Develop a Play-to-Earn (P2E) Game?
Items.Description:
创建一个像样的游戏的先决条件因游戏类型而异。 谈到赚钱游戏，投资者和玩家都必须考虑在内。 与典型模型不同的是，这种情况下的“投资者”是玩家自己。 因此，想要制作一款体面游戏的个人必须首先确定他们正在为“谁”创建游戏。

我们从之前的系统中了解到，传统投资者会评估游戏的收入潜力。 这条古老的规则仍然适用于游戏中，并有一些额外的补充。 鉴于玩家也是投资者，因此在开发区块链游戏时，创作者具有很大的优势。 你也可以成为这个行业的一部分。 在这里您可以获得p2e 游戏开发方面的帮助。

你应该为谁创造游戏？

在其他情况下，我们会看到玩起来并不愉快但产生巨额利润的游戏。 这是因为开发商创造游戏是为了满足传统投资者的需求。 了解投资者审查的价值后，开发人员可以创建他们认为可以通过所有性能测试的游戏。 当游戏通过这些标准时，它可能会吸引投资者的兴趣并获得资金。

看到以这种方式构建并赚钱的游戏，其他开发人员希望创建遵循类似路线的游戏。 缺乏有效的游戏机制、保持用户参与的材料以及此类游戏的强大玩家社区的游戏的成功通常转瞬即逝。 即使有资金，这类游戏也不会成功，它们让玩家和投资者都失望。

制作“玩家友好型”游戏

仅仅为了满足投资者而创造的游戏的长期成功是非常具有挑战性的。 那么，“为玩家创造游戏”是什么意思，它是如何影响成功的呢？

制作一款好游戏并让参与者开心的方法如下：

游戏的主要机制应该是扎实的

游戏机制细分为子流派，基本机制构成游戏玩法（gameplay）。 游戏最基本的目标是在玩游戏时获得乐趣。 否则谁会在游戏开始时投入数小时或考虑在游戏上花钱？ 认识到这个国家的娱乐因人而异，这一点至关重要。 一些游戏玩家喜欢动作游戏，而另一些则喜欢恐怖游戏。

游戏要有一个不沉闷的核心循环

一般来说，游戏中的关键机制数量很少。 当您查看大多数游戏时，您会发现这些基本技术被反复使用。 这被称为核心循环。 如果玩家觉得他们在整个循环中一遍又一遍地执行相同的事情，他们可能会感到无聊。 开发者必须加强这个循环，以防止玩家感到无聊。

游戏的经济性应该是平衡的

单人游戏也具有游戏内经济。 在讨论经济时，不要只考虑金钱、黄金或钻石。 在最基本的形式中，经济可以被认为是资源/投入支出/产出。 当你这样想时，游戏中的一切都会影响游戏的平衡。 您需要继续玩的 HP、能量或任何其他类似的游戏机制也可能计入游戏经济。 归根结底，它们也可以作为一种现金。 归根结底，它们充当游戏现金的一种形式。 玩赚钱游戏和其他类型的游戏之间的区别在于，外部资源被整合到经济中。

在 Earn-to-Play 游戏中包含 NFT 和加密元素

我们说过，游戏赚钱游戏包含外部资源。 我们在这里要讨论的不是技术集成，而是应该如何将加密方面纳入到博弈的逻辑中。

在区块链游戏中，NFT 被用于各种目标。 在一些游戏中，通常被称为“头像”的 NFT 被用作游戏内商品。 拥有 NFT 的玩家从这个 NFT 中获利这一事实是继续玩游戏的强大动力。

设计一款好游戏的黄金准则，无论类型如何，都是平衡时间和体验。 如果用户在玩游戏的时间里没有获得足够的经验，他就会放弃它。 没有玩过的游戏就是已经死去的游戏。

在游戏中，能够平衡时间和体验是至关重要的。 游戏的类型决定了如何实现这种平衡。 游戏兑现承诺至关重要。

另请阅读此处了解供应链区块链的发展。



原文:

The prerequisites for creating a decent game differ depending on the sort of game. When it comes to play-to-earn games, both investors and players must be considered. Unlike typical models, the "investors" in this case are the players themselves. As a result, individuals who wish to build a decent game must first identify "for whom" they are creating the game.

We know from prior systems that traditional investors assess the game's revenue potential. This ancient rule still applies in play-to-earn games, with a few additional additions. Given that the players are also investors, creators have a big advantage when developing a blockchain game. You can also become part of this industry. Here you can get help with p2e game development.

For whom should you create games?

In other circumstances, we see games that are not really pleasurable to play yet generate enormous profits. This is due to the fact that developers create games in response to the desires of conventional investors. Knowing the values that investors examine, developers create games that they believe will pass all performance tests. When the game passes these standards, it might attract investors' interest and acquire funding.

Seeing games that have been built and made money in this manner, other developers desire to create games that follow a similar route. The success of games that lack effective game mechanics, material that keeps the user engaged, and a robust player community for the sort of game is generally fleeting. Even with funding, these sorts of games do not succeed, and they disappoint both players and investors.

Making "Player-Friendly" Games

Long-term success of games created just to satisfy investors is very challenging. So, what does it mean to "create a game for the player," and how does it effect success?

What it takes to produce a good game and keep the participants happy is as follows:

The main mechanics of the game should be solid

Game mechanics are subdivided into subgenres, and basic mechanics comprise the gameplay (gameplay). The most fundamental goal of a game is to have pleasure while playing it. Who would otherwise invest hours at the start of a game or contemplate spending money on it? It is crucial to realize that entertainment in this country differs from individual to person. Some gamers like action games, while others favor horror games.

The game must have a core loop that is not dull

In general, the number of key mechanisms in games is small. When you look at most games, you'll see that these fundamental techniques are repeatedly used. This is known as a core loop. If the player feels like they are performing the same thing over and over again throughout this cycle, they will likely get bored. Developers must enhance this cycle in order to prevent the player from becoming bored.

The game's economics should be balanced

Single-player games, too, feature an in-game economy. When discussing the economy, don't merely think about money, gold, or diamonds. In its most basic form, the economy may be thought of as resource/input expenditure/output. Everything in the game impacts the game's balance when you think like that. Your HP, energy, or any other comparable game mechanic that you require to keep playing may also be tallied into the game's economy. At the end of the day, they also serve as a kind of cash. At the end of the day, they serve as a form of game cash. The distinction between play-to-earn games and other types of games is that an outside source is integrated in the economy.

Including NFTs and Crypto Elements in Earn-to-Play Games

We said that the play-to-earn games incorporate an external source. What we shall discuss here is not technology integration, but rather how crypto aspects should be included into the logic of a play-to-win game.

In blockchain games, NFTs are utilized for a variety of objectives. In several games, NFTs, often known as "profile images," are utilized as in-game commodities. The fact that the player who owns NFT profits from this NFT serves as a strong incentive to keep playing the game.

The golden guideline of designing a good game, regardless of genre, is to balance time and experience. If the user does not get enough experience for the time spent playing the game, he will abandon it. A game that has not been played is a game that has died.

It is critical in games to be able to balance time and experience. The kind of game determines how this balance is accomplished. It is critical that the games deliver on their promises.

Items.Title: 第一周积极使用社交媒体和博客 | First Week Actively Using Social Media and Blogging
Items.Description:
目标是在社交媒体上更加活跃并开始写博客。 今天我将分享我的经验和我的未来计划。 如果这篇文章对读者感兴趣，我会写更多这样的文章。

挑战之路

动力的运气让我想知道，如何保持动力而不是筋疲力尽。 动力正在下降，因为真的很难找到工作。 在大多数情况下，在发送我的简历后，我被拒绝了。 因此，通过面试很难获得经验。


有趣的时刻，当我遇到技术问题或任务时，这比回答 HR 的问题要容易得多。 其中一个问题是——“天空是什么颜色的，为什么”。 这个问题并不难，但在我回答了一个愚蠢的问题后，我正在回答另一个问题。 我很困惑，因为我期望 HR 会问这样的问题：“你如何解决冲突？”，或者“你喜欢团队合作还是单独工作？”等等。这个问题很愚蠢，因为任何开发人员都会解决问题，并创造一些东西通过使用特定技术。 今天我可以说这是我经历过的最糟糕的采访。 在其他公司，人力资源部试图了解我的软技能是否符合他们的要求。 后来我的朋友分享了他的面试问题。 那是——“猫还是狗？”，我的朋友正在申请工作，这需要网络开发技能。 在经历了这样的面试之后，我的动力非常低落，信心几乎为零，直到我找到了一份条件不错的第一份工作。

我有一个 LinkedIn 帐户

立陶宛的 LinkedIn 是我见过的最没用的服务之一。 我从 CodeAcademy BootCamp 获得了一些针对 LinkedIn 的提示，但它根本没有帮助。 大多数对找工作有任何了解的人告诉我，这项服务在立陶宛无效。 自由职业呢？ 我想向专业人士学习以获得更多经验。

改变的理由？

从学校开始，我在社交媒体上并不活跃。 我在想这是一种无用的浪费时间。 直到 2022 年 6 月 13 日，我才决定改变我的生活方式。 如果我早点这样做，我会有更多的关系。 在这种情况下，找到工作的机会会更好。 此外，写博客不仅可以帮助我拥有更多的网络，还可以帮助我成长为一名专业人士并提高我的英语技能。

社交媒体

我开始使用 Instagram 和 Twitter。 开始在 LinkedIn 上花费比以前更多的时间。 就目前而言，Instagram 并不是开发人员分享想法的最佳社交媒体。 也许是因为我对正确使用它的了解不够。 我正在阅读标签并在实践中使用它们。 为了得出结论，我需要更多时间。 每天我都会发现一些有趣和有价值的东西来阅读。 人们非常活跃和友好。 也许是因为这是我的第一印象，而且是积极的。

开发者

免责声明，信息在 6 月 23 日之前提供。我很忙，因此，查看次数比 dev.to 上的应有数量多 20 到 50 次。 用户体验是最伟大的之一。 它使用简单，对 facebook 来说看起来很熟悉。

哈希节点

我喜欢 Hashnode 主页 UI。 它显示了我需要的所有内容，而且，它还可以让您更轻松地关注即将到来的黑客马拉松。 统计数据比中等好一点。

中等的

后来我找到了其他平台，并通过添加规范链接将 Medium 用作我的主要平台。 在这种情况下，SEO 搜索会更好，因为它解决了内容重复问题。 在 dev.to 文章中可以找到——“最初发表于 Medium”。 与其他平台相比，我在 Medium 上的文章的统计数据最差。

目前，没有文章的统计数据。 出于这个原因，我将显示我所拥有的通知。 这个平台对我来说很有希望。 一年后，我相信他们会为读者和内容创作者做出很多很棒的更新。



原文:

On June 13 I started changing my lifestyle. The goal was to be more active on social media and start blogging. Today I will share my experience and my future plans. If this article will be interesting to readers, I will write more this kind of article.

Path of Challenges

Luck of motivation made me wonder, how to stay motivated and not burn out. The motivation was falling because it was really hard to find work. In most cases, after sending my CV I was rejected. For this reason, it was hard to earn experience to pass the interview.


Funny moment, when I had technical questions or tasks it was way easier than answering questions from HR. One of these kinds of questions was — “What color of the sky and why”. The question is not hard, but after I had answered one silly question I was answering another. I was confused because I expected HR questions like: “How do You solve conflicts?”, or” do You like to work in teams, or individually?”, etc. The question is silly because any developer solves the problem, and creates something by using specific technologies. Today I can say it was the worst interview, I ever had. In the other companies, HR was trying to understand if my soft skills match their requirements. Later my friend shared his interview question. It was — “cat or dog?”, my friend was applying to work, that requires web development skills. After having this kind of interviews, I had a lot of demotivation and almost zero confidence until I found my first job on good terms.

I Had a LinkedIn Account

LinkedIn in Lithuania is one of the most useless services, that I have ever seen. I had some tips from CodeAcademy BootCamp for LinkedIn, but it not helped at all. Most people, who had any knowledge of finding work told me, that this service is not effective in Lithuania. What about freelancing? I wanted to learn from professionals to earn more experience.

Reason to Change?

From school, I was not active on social media. I was thinking it is a useless waste of time. It wasn’t until 2022 June 13 that I decided to change my lifestyle. If I would do it earlier I would have had more relationships. In this case, the chances to find a job would be better. Moreover, blogging could not only help me to have more networks but also grow as a professional and improve my English skills.

Social Media

I started using Instagram and Twitter. Started spending more time on LinkedIn than before. The situation on LinkedIn has not changed at all. Instagram for now, is not the best social media for developers to share their minds. Maybe because I do not know enough to use it properly. I was reading about tags and using them in practice. To make a conclusion I will need more time. Twitter is the best for me. Every day I find something interesting and valuable to read. People are very active and friendly. Maybe because this is my first impression and it is positive.

Dev.to

Disclaimer, info is provided by June 23. I was busy, and for this reason, the number of views is 20–50 more than it should be on dev.to. UX is one of the greatest. It is simple in use and looks familiar to facebook.

Hashnode

I love the Hashnode homepage UI. It shows all, that I need Also, makes it easier to follow upcoming hackathons. Stats are a little bit better than Medium.

Medium

Medium is the first platform where I started publishing my first articles. Later I found other platforms and used Medium as my main platform by adding canonical links. In this case, SEO search will be working better, because it solves the content duplication problems. In dev.to articles can find — “Originally published at Medium”. Compared to other platforms my articles on Medium have the worst statistics.

Tealfeed This platform is newer than others. For now, there are no stats for articles. For this reason, I will show notifications, of what I have. This platform looks promising to me. After a year I am sure they will make a lot of great updates for readers and content creators.

Items.Title: 函数的基本理解 | Basic understanding of Functions
Items.Description:
基兰菲利普斯

发表于功能

当您如此专注于学习新事物时，您是否曾经在生活中产生过这种感觉，但是当您学习时，您的大脑正在尝试处理太多事情，以至于无法理解传递给您的更复杂的信息。 相信我，你不是一个人，最近我做了我们很多人说我们希望做的事情，那就是学习一门语言。 我不是在这里告诉你我现在可以说西班牙语或荷兰语，但令我惊讶的是，我开始学习软件工程师说 JavaScript 的语言。

现在，在学习一门语言时，您必须学习许多不同的因素，例如方言或天气过去现在或未来。 那就是你可能会问...... 一个函数。

函数是 JavaScript 中最重要的代码单元。 这是因为它们用作将 JavaScript 代码的相关位组合在一起的方式。 分组代码更易于阅读、调试和改进。 必须知道的是，为了在 JavaScript 中“执行”或“调用”一个函数，您需要在其名称后添加 ()。 当你调用一个函数时，它需要返回一些东西。 让我们考虑点餐，如果当我去限制时，我饿了，我告诉女服务员我想要一个汉堡，如果你在一个函数中想到这一点，这个函数就是下订单，返回的是你的汉堡. 然而，情况并非总是如此，函数可以但没有义务在执行结束时返回返回值。 返回值通常是过程的结果、总计或成功/失败数据。 所以，可能是去餐馆找工作，但他们不能给你一份工作，所以你一无所获。

在 JavaScript 中编写函数有 3 种方法，即函数声明、函数表达式和箭头函数

函数声明是定义函数的传统方式。 它在某种程度上类似于我们在其他编程语言中定义函数的方式。 我们开始使用关键字“function”进行声明。 然后我们写函数名，然后是参数。

函数表达式是在 JavaScript 中定义函数的另一种方式。 在这里，我们使用变量定义一个函数并将返回值存储在该变量中。

添加(2, 3);

箭头函数是在 ES6 版本的 JavaScript 中引入的。 它用于缩短代码。 这里我们不使用“function”关键字，而是使用箭头符号。

添加(2, 3);

很容易理解是的，我知道是对的，但是嘿，学习一门语言什么时候这么容易。 所以现在我可以告诉你事情可能会变得有点复杂你知道函数的基础知识以及它的作用但是如果我告诉你你可以在函数中传递一个函数作为参数。 是的，没错，这个术语称为嵌套。

我们称之为回调，回调是一个函数，您可以将其作为参数传递给另一个函数，以便稍后执行。 让我们更深入地了解回调，您将获得两种类型的同步和异步。 在执行使用回调的高阶函数期间执行同步回调。 但是，让我们切换原因 在执行使用回调的高阶函数之后执行异步回调。 同步性意味着如果 JavaScript 必须等待一个操作完成，它将在等待时执行其余的代码。

总结一下，不要让您对我们今天所经历的一切失去意义，您现在应该了解函数的基础知识、函数的类型以及您能够拥有回调函数，并通过这些函数中的每一个来帮助您获得一些见解到每个函数使用的基础知识。 快乐编码

Functions

Have you ever had that feeling in life when so focused on learning something new, but as you learning your mind is trying to process so much it’s not making sense of the more complex information being passed on to you. Well believe me you not alone recently I have done what many of us say we hope to do and that is learn a language. I’m not here telling you I can now speak Spanish or Dutch but to my surprise I took up learning the language that software engineers speak JavaScript.

Now when learning a language there are many different factors you must learn such as dialect or weather its past present or future. JavaScript is slightly different here you learn how it consists of so many different elements, I’m here to tell you what I found to be a vital element that will be used always think of it as one of the core elements. That is you may ask…. A function.

Functions are the single most important unit of code in JavaScript. This is because they serve as ways to group together related bits of JavaScript code. Grouped code is easier to read, debug, and improve. What must be know is that in order to "execute" or "call" a function in JavaScript you add () after its name. when you call a function, it will need to return something. Let’s think of ordering a meal if when I go to a restraint, I am hungry and I tell the waitress I would love a burger and if you think of this in a function the function would be placing the order and the return would be your burger. However, this is not the case always Functions can, but are not obligated to, return return values at the end of their execution. Return values are often results of a process, grand totals, or success / failure data. So, it may be the case where go to a restaurant ask for a job but they are not able to give you one so you getting nothing in return.

There are 3 ways in writing a function in JavaScript being Function Declaration, Function expression and arrow function

Function Declaration is the traditional way to define a function. It is somehow similar to the way we define a function in other programming languages. We start declaring using the keyword “function”. Then we write the function name and then parameters.

// Function declaration function add(a, b) { console.log(a + b);

}

// Calling a function add(2, 3

Function Expression is another way to define a function in JavaScript. Here we define a function using a variable and store the returned value in that variable.

// Function Expression const add = function(a, b) { console.log(a+b);

}

// Calling function
add(2, 3);

Arrow Function are been introduced in the ES6 version of JavaScript. It is used to shorten the code. Here we do not use the “function” keyword and use the arrow symbol.

// Function Expression const add = function(a, b) { console.log(a+b);

}

// Calling function
add(2, 3);

Pretty simple to understand yeah, I know right but hey when is learning a language ever that easy. so now I can tell where things may get a little complicated you know the basics of a function and what it does but what If I told you that you can pass a function within a function as an argument. Yup that’s right and the term for this is called nested.

what we call this is a callback and a callback is a function that you pass into another function as an argument for executing later. Lets dive a little deeper with callbacks you get two types synchronous and also asynchronous. A synchronous callback is executed during the execution of the high-order function that uses the callback. The isOdd and isEven are examples of synchronous callbacks because they execute during the execution of the filter() function. However lets switch that around cause An asynchronous callback is executed after the execution of the high-order function that uses the callback. A synchronicity means that if JavaScript must wait for an operation to complete, it will execute the rest of the code while waiting.

This is a video link to watch what a callback is in plain terms.
https://youtu.be/AUCavCH7FTw

to sum this up and and not let you lose sense of what we have gone through today you should now know the basics of functions the type of functions and that you are able to have callback functions and through each of these to help you gain some insight to the basics of each use of a function. happy coding
